what_it_does: A standalone HTML test page that renders two decision-card archetype examples side by side: a pending four-option row variant and a decided four-option variant with "From the tree" selected.
observations: Defines a centered comparison row with captions for pending and decided states.
Loads shared surface CSS, phone CSS, Google Fonts, and /surface/runtime/archetype.js.
Fetches /dna/layouts.json and uses layouts.archetypes["decision-card"] with DNA.renderArchetype.
Builds local decision records with identity metadata, four options, implications, a recommended option, and optional decided_value.
Provides a resolver that supplies explanation_source text and state values to the renderer.
standouts: The file is structured as a visual state comparison for one archetype variant.
The pending and decided examples share the same generated record shape with only state and decided_value changing.
The caption text explicitly describes the intended ledger-row grammar and decided-state treatment.
conventions: Uses inline page-specific CSS around shared surface and phone styles.
Uses an async immediately invoked function for setup.
Keeps test data local to the page rather than loading records from a fixture.
Uses mount nodes to separate explanatory captions from rendered archetype output.
concerns: The document title says "v2" while the path names a v7 row variant.
The script assumes fetch, layouts.json, the decision-card archetype, and global DNA are available, with no local failure state.
The caption term "scr-ledger" is not defined within this file.
notes: This file is a file-local visual fixture for comparing pending and decided multi-option decision-card row behavior.
questions: Is "scr-ledger" an intentional shorthand or a typo?
Should the title version match the v7 path name?
Is the decided full-card gold-ring behavior implemented by the archetype renderer or only described here?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it sets up pending and decided four-option decision-card examples and captions the row-variant behavior being tested.
intention_signals: It appears intended to exercise and document how a decision-card renders 3+ options as ledger-style rows, including how a chosen row remains marked after the decision state changes.
novelty: The page pairs pending and decided instances of the same four-option record to show both row layout and selection persistence in one fixture.
contribution: This file contributes a compact decision-card fixture with local multi-option decision data, explanatory copy, and paired pending/decided render targets for preserving row-variant behavior in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML fixture rendering pending and decided four-option decision-card archetype examples using local records and DNA.renderArchetype.
intention_for_embedding: Created to show and test the ledger-row treatment for multi-option decision cards across pending and decided states.
